_meta.note: Virginia high-value federal/statewide hygiene + evidence pass. (1) Mark Warner: fix stale 'RETIRING 2026' note — he is seeking a 4th term; re-score key cells with cited votes. (2) Hung Cao: fix stale '2026 R Senate candidate' — he is Acting Secretary of the Navy (assumed 2026-04-22); candidacy_status not_running; re-score 2024 campaign positions with evidence, null unsupported archetype TRUEs. (3) Tim Kaine: replace thin all-False archetype with cited evidence + null unproven cells. (4) Eugene Vindman: correct Laken Riley dual-vote inconsistency (first House vote NAY 2025-01-07; later House concurrence YEA 2025-01-22 per The Hill/clerk); preserve other evidence. (5) Jennifer McClellan: strengthen sparse cells with SAVE Act / Laken Riley / life cites; no invented votes. reset_unspecified only where prior scores were archetype bulk (Kaine, Warner, Hung Cao).

tim-kaine.notes_set: U.S. Senator (D-VA) since 2013; 2016 Democratic VP nominee; former VA Governor (2006–2010). Catholic; progressive voting record on social/cultural issues. [2026-07-18 RESOLUTE VA refinement, federal tier] HARD evidence: Heritage Action lifetime ~3% / session 0% (119th). Cosponsored and voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act (2022) codifying federal recognition of same-sex marriage. NAY on Laken Riley Act final Senate passage (S.5, Record Vote 7, 2025-01-20) after calling the bill 'bad policy.' Introduced Age 21 Act (2025) raising minimum age to buy assault weapons/high-capacity magazines to 21; long-standing gun-control portfolio and NRA 'F' (which he has publicly touted). SBA Pro-Life America scorecard documents opposition to defunding Planned Parenthood / Medicaid abortion-related measures. Progressive life/marriage cells scored FALSE only where documented; embryonic/IVF, euthanasia, school-choice specifics, CBDC/Fed, most industry_capture cells left NULL (no citable individual vote located this pass). foreign_policy_restraint: prior TRUE on war-powers/AUMF cells preserved only where already documented elsewhere in pipeline; AIPAC-linked cells left as prior or null without re-audit of OpenSecrets this pass.

mark-warner.notes_set: U.S. Senator (D-VA) since 2009; former VA Governor; among the Senate's wealthiest members. SEEKING a fourth term in 2026 (NOT retiring — prior scorecard note was stale/wrong). Democratic nominee after sole Dem filing; faces GOP primary winner among Kim Farington, Bert Mizusawa, David Williams (Aug 4, 2026 primary). [2026-07-18 RESOLUTE VA refinement, federal tier] HARD evidence: voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act (2022) with public statement praising same-sex marriage legal recognition. Co-introduced 'Virginia Plan to Reduce Gun Violence Act' (2026) exporting state-level gun restrictions federally (AWB/red-flag-direction package with Kaine). YEA on Laken Riley Act Senate passage (S.5, Record Vote 7, 2025-01-20) — moderate break with many Dems, documented in Senate roll call and The Hill; still does not equal support for mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens (q1 left null, not TRUE). Progressive life/marriage baseline from RFMA + long Dem record; null cells where no specific vote located. Prior thin all-False archetype cleared.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-07-18 RESOLUTE VA refinement: CRITICAL status fix — record said '2026 R Candidate vs Warner' but Ballotpedia/DefenseScoop/Wikipedia confirm he is Acting Secretary of the Navy (assumed 2026-04-22) after serving as Navy undersecretary nominee. Not on 2026 VA Senate ballot (R field is Farington/Mizusawa/Williams). Scores now evidence-based from 2024 Senate campaign statements; archetype all-TRUE cleared; null where no citable position.

hung-cao-senate-2026.notes_set: Acting U.S. Secretary of the Navy (assumed Apr 22, 2026 per DefenseScoop/Ballotpedia/Wikipedia). Retired Navy captain; 2022 R nominee VA-10 (lost); 2024 R Senate nominee (lost to Tim Kaine). [2026-07-18 RESOLUTE VA refinement] STATUS HYGIENE: prior record incorrectly listed him as 2026 R Senate candidate vs Warner — he is NOT running for Senate in 2026 (that R primary is Farington/Mizusawa/Williams). Trump admin Navy role is current public office. SCORES are from documented 2024 campaign positions (still the best public policy record), not invented votes: self-described pro-life ('life begins at conception'; thrilled by Dobbs) but explicitly said he would NOT vote for a federal abortion ban (states decide) — so q0 TRUE, q1 FALSE (not abolition). Other marquee 2024 issue pages (border, guns, election integrity, CRT/DEI) existed on hungforva.com but page body text was not fully recoverable this pass — those cells left NULL rather than retain uncited archetype TRUEs. foreign_policy AIPAC cell prior FALSE preserved only if re-sourced; left null. This is a status+honesty pass: better to show sparse documented scores than fake 97% from party defaults.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-07-18 RESOLUTE VA pass: corrected Laken Riley dual-vote facts. House Roll 6 (H.R.29, 2025-01-07): Vindman NAY (own press release + clerk roll006.xml). House Roll 23 (2025-01-22 concurrence after Senate): Vindman YEA (The Hill list of Dem YEAs citing clerk Votes/202523; Prince William Times: first no, later yes). Prior confidence_note wrongly said YEA on first vote. Border cells: keep enforcement-skeptical scoring; notes document both votes. Other 2026-06 evidence (SAVE NAY, WHPA cosponsor, parental-rights YES H.R.2616, Iran war powers YES, LOCAL Foods, etc.) preserved — no reset_unspecified.

eugene-vindman.notes_append: [2026-07-18 RESOLUTE VA correction] Laken Riley dual-vote hygiene: (1) Jan 7, 2025 first legislative vote as Member — NAY on H.R.29 (House Roll 6 / roll006.xml; vindman.house.gov: 'I voted no today to uphold the right to due process'). (2) Jan 22, 2025 House concurrence after Senate — YEA (The Hill, citing clerk Votes/202523, lists Eugene Vindman among Dem YEAs; Prince William Times: 'Vindman first voted no on the bill but then later voted yes'). Prior internal confidence_note that said simple YEA on Laken Riley was incomplete/wrong for the first vote. Broader immigration posture still enforcement-skeptical (opposed Stafford ICE detention center; due-process framing) so border_immigration hard TRUE not warranted; existing FALSEs/nulls preserved. No other cells rescaled this pass.

1.note: First vote NAY on Laken Riley + later opposition to Stafford ICE mega-detention funding; public framing prioritizes due process and legal pathways over mandatory mass deportation of all illegal aliens.
